refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall maintain teleoperation communication quality such that packet loss does not exceed the customer-defined limit under agreed network conditions.
refined_fr.rationale: Refining the original FR clarifies the performance metric (packet loss) and ties it explicitly to customer-defined limits and network conditions, ensuring measurable and testable requirements.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure packet loss percentage during teleoperation sessions under controlled network conditions and verify compliance with the customer-defined limit.
sub_frs: FR1: Monitor network packet loss in real-time during teleoperation.
FR2: Adapt teleoperation communication parameters to maintain packet loss within limits.
FR3: Report packet loss status and alerts to relevant stakeholders.

candidate_dps: DP1: Implement real-time packet loss monitoring and feedback control in teleoperation communication stack.
DP2: Use adaptive communication protocols (e.g., error correction, retransmission) to reduce effective packet loss.
DP3: Provide packet loss status reporting via telemetry interface.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Implement real-time packet loss monitoring and feedback control in teleoperation communication stack.

coupling_check.notes: The selected DP logically isolates packet loss monitoring and control within the teleoperation communication stack, minimizing coupling with unrelated system functions.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions are within agreed parameters for effective control.
Risk that packet loss monitoring latency may impact control responsiveness.
Risk that adaptive control may conflict with safety policies if not properly integrated.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating adaptive communication protocols (DP2) in future refinement to enhance robustness.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for teleoperation communication, control, and monitoring.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Interface for sending and receiving teleoperation data packets subject to packet loss.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Interface to report packet loss status and alerts to NGHE Solution and other stakeholders.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives teleoperation commands that depend on communication quality.

module_containment_scores.notes: High-level system module; contains overall coordination but not detailed teleoperation communication logic.
Physical vehicle module; limited direct involvement in teleoperation communication control.
Primary module for teleoperation communication; best containment and minimal diffusion.
Safety monitoring may receive alerts but does not implement communication control.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Assigning the DP to the Teleoperation module centralizes communication quality control, minimizing diffusion and enabling clear ownership.
containment_risks: Potential integration challenges with Safety Services if safety policies require overrides.
Risk of interface overload if Teleoperation module handles too many responsibilities.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Monitor interface complexity and consider modularizing packet loss monitoring subcomponents if diffusion increases.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The refined FR is clearly addressed by the selected DP, which logically implements real-time packet loss monitoring and control.

diffusion_risks: Potential diffusion if Teleoperation module grows too complex with added communication control responsibilities.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Teleoperation module shows highest containment score and minimal diffusion, supporting good architectural packaging.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed by testing packet loss metrics via Teleoperation module interfaces under controlled network conditions.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of responsibility to Teleoperation module with well-defined external interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Plan for integration testing with Safety Services to ensure safety policies are respected.
Consider future refinement to include adaptive communication protocols for enhanced robustness.

decision.justification: The current level of functional decomposition, DP selection, and IM assignment satisfy the requirement with clear ownership, manageable coupling, and feasible verification.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Monitor network packet loss in real-time during teleoperation.
FR2: Adapt teleoperation communication parameters to maintain packet loss within limits.
FR3: Report packet loss status and alerts to relevant stakeholders.

revise.why: No immediate revision needed; next step is to proceed to next decomposition level.

assumptions: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters.
Teleoperation module can handle additional communication control responsibilities.
open_risks: Integration with Safety Services for safety-critical overrides.
evidence_needed: Packet loss measurement data under representative network conditions.
Verification of Teleoperation module's ability to maintain packet loss within limits.
